internet-access.md.question: What does the author mean by "monotone" management?
internet-access.md.answer: The author uses the term "monotone" to describe a management style where a manager assumes all employees are either fully independent ("big boys/girls") or require massive micromanagement ("complete ninnies"), showing a rare ability to adapt or shift gears between the two.

internet-access.md.question: What is the difference between problem solvers and quota workers as described in the post?
internet-access.md.answer: Problem solvers are those who figure out complex issues with very few instructions and are left to work independently until a deadline or milestone. Quota workers, on the other hand, follow a detailed workflow with expected outputs over a period of time and face more frequent deadlines.

internet-access.md.question: What observation made the author wonder if Internet access is not as productive as it could be?
internet-access.md.answer: The author questioned the productivity of Internet access after seeing an overflowing bin reserved for printed phone books at the recycling bins, wondering why phone books still exist if the Internet is highly productive.

smooth-k8s-operator.md.question: How old is the Kubernetes Operators concept according to the blog post?
smooth-k8s-operator.md.answer: The Kubernetes Operators concept is seven (7) years old, tracing back to its introduction in 2016.

smooth-k8s-operator.md.question: In which section of the Kubernetes documentation can the canonical definition of Operators be found?
smooth-k8s-operator.md.answer: The canonical definition of Operators is located in the "Extending" section of the Kubernetes documentation.

smooth-k8s-operator.md.question: What is the primary purpose of using Kubernetes Operators?
smooth-k8s-operator.md.answer: Kubernetes Operators are used to extract and apply critical, customized operational knowledge to automate human-intensive tasks associated with application deployment, lifecycle, care, and feeding.

a-spotlight-on-ai-on-devices.md.question: What does the acronym "DPWCG" stand for, as proposed by the author?
a-spotlight-on-ai-on-devices.md.answer: The acronym "DPWCG" stands for deterministic power, weight, cooling, and geometry, which refers to a growing list of A.I. initiatives focused on these criteria.

a-spotlight-on-ai-on-devices.md.question: What is Keep?
a-spotlight-on-ai-on-devices.md.answer: Keep is an open-source alert management and automation tool that provides everything you need to create and manage alerts effectively.

a-spotlight-on-ai-on-devices.md.question: What tool did the author write about in February 2023 for Wardley mapping, and how can users access it now?
a-spotlight-on-ai-on-devices.md.answer: The author wrote about mapkeep.com, an online tool for strategy mapping. Currently, users who want to create their own maps can join the waitlist on their website.

the-promised-landing-zones.md.question: What alternative terms does the blog post suggest using to describe Landing Zones (LZ)?
the-promised-landing-zones.md.answer: The blog post suggests replacing the term Landing Zones (LZ) with plans, blueprints, or recipes.

the-promised-landing-zones.md.question: What common elements are included in Landing Zone discussions and definitions for Cloud Service Providers (CSPs)?
the-promised-landing-zones.md.answer: Landing Zone definitions combine technical and business decisions, including: considerations for governance that are well-architected with industry-standard best practices; application and platform specific security profiles with multi-account isolation methodology; a modular design for scale, management, and lifecycle; and a summary of a vast number of lessons learned.

the-promised-landing-zones.md.question: How does the author use the pasta sauce analogy to describe different Landing Zone requirements?
the-promised-landing-zones.md.answer: The author compares different Landing Zones to pasta sauces to show how different application deployments have different needs, such as using a zesty arrabiata for Kubernetes (k8s), an alfredo for an ML/AI stack, or a basic pomodoro for a legacy monolith.

cloud-data-services-ahead.md.question: What company did the author of the blog post join, and what is their new role?
cloud-data-services-ahead.md.answer: The author joined Faction in a new servant leader role.

cloud-data-services-ahead.md.question: According to the post, what is the definition of Cloud Data Services?
cloud-data-services-ahead.md.answer: Cloud Data Services is a category to describe the common elements associated with managed delivery of data services that exist across a continuous spectrum from Private Cloud to IaaS / PaaS / FaaS / SaaS within Public Cloud as well as Hybrid Cloud.

cloud-data-services-ahead.md.question: What products back Faction's Cloud Data Services for Multi-Cloud practice?
cloud-data-services-ahead.md.answer: Faction's practice is backed by Cloud Control Volumes™ (CCV™) and Hybrid Disaster Recovery-as-a-Service (HDRaaS) products, which are enabled by a growing number of patents.

make-iiot-easy-on-yourself.md.question: What do the acronyms RRoA and RPoA stand for?
make-iiot-easy-on-yourself.md.answer: RRoA stands for the 'Ruthless Removal of Annoyances' and RPoA stands for the 'Relentless Pursuit of Automation'.

make-iiot-easy-on-yourself.md.question: What is the purpose of The Open Group Open Process Automation Standard (O-PAS)?
make-iiot-easy-on-yourself.md.answer: O-PAS seeks to bring 'federated process automation systems' to all industrial plants, impacting everything from mining to chemical processing.

make-iiot-easy-on-yourself.md.question: How does the author define the concept of 'cloud'?
make-iiot-easy-on-yourself.md.answer: The author defines the cloud as an 'operating model' rather than a 'place'.

artemis-radiation-hardening-and-ipfs.md.question: What are the primary objectives of NASA's Artemis missions?
artemis-radiation-hardening-and-ipfs.md.answer: According to the post, NASA's Artemis missions aim to land the first woman and first person of color on the Moon, collaborate with commercial and international partners to establish the first long-term presence on the Moon, and utilize the lessons learned on and around the Moon to send the first astronauts to Mars.

artemis-radiation-hardening-and-ipfs.md.question: What is the RAD750 computer used in the James Webb Space Telescope, and how does it compare to a Raspberry Pi 4?
artemis-radiation-hardening-and-ipfs.md.answer: The RAD750 is a radiation-hardened single-board computer based on PowerPC 750 running at 118 MHz that was first released in 2001 and costs $200,000. By comparison, a $35 Raspberry Pi 4 released in 2019 is approximately 35 times faster.

artemis-radiation-hardening-and-ipfs.md.question: Which company is planning to demonstrate space-based use cases for IPFS, and what analogy is used to describe it?
artemis-radiation-hardening-and-ipfs.md.answer: Lockheed Martin will soon publicly demonstrate non-terrestrial convergence enabling decentralization use cases for IPFS (Interplanetary File System) to support in-space economies. The author analogizes IPFS as an open way to enable an 'Akamai in the Sky' on planets and exoplanets.

clean-my-bookmarks.md.question: What happened to the social-bookmarking site Del.icio.us?
clean-my-bookmarks.md.answer: Del.icio.us was shut down almost five years ago after being acquired by Pinboard, another social-bookmarking site.

clean-my-bookmarks.md.question: What formatting changes does the author plan to make to their newsletter?
clean-my-bookmarks.md.answer: The author plans to drop Spotify playlist updates, remove the 'Work Plug' section, transition to workflows driven increasingly by Obsidian and automation, and likely shift embedded rich card URLs from mikrolink to an alternative.

clean-my-bookmarks.md.question: Who are the interview participants in the first reading suggestion about Spotify?
clean-my-bookmarks.md.answer: The first reading suggestion features Jennifer Riggins interviewing Helen Greul on how Spotify achieved a voluntary 99% internal platform adoption rate for Backstage.

social-telecom-2030.md.question: What does the acronym ESTEEM stand for according to the blog post?
social-telecom-2030.md.answer: According to the author, ESTEEM stands for STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math) plus Ethics and Empathy.

social-telecom-2030.md.question: What change did Delta Airlines make to its Twitter presence in early 2016?
social-telecom-2030.md.answer: Delta Airlines phased out its dedicated @DeltaAssist account on Twitter in early 2016 to simply use its main @Delta account.

social-telecom-2030.md.question: How does the author compare cloud egress fees of the 2020s to past telecom charges?
social-telecom-2030.md.answer: The author describes cloud egress fees of the 2020s as the modern version of long-distance telephone charges from the 1990s, predicting they will become completely unjustifiable by 2030.

zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2022.md.question: How many posts did the Fudge Sunday newsletter publish in 2022?
zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2022.md.answer: Fudge Sunday published approximately 50 posts in 2022.

zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2022.md.question: Where does the author organize the online resources they read?
zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2022.md.answer: The author organizes online resources as files in Obsidian, and also adds to their Reading List or bookmarks all of their browser tabs.

zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2022.md.question: What new section is the author considering experimenting with for the newsletter next year?
zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2022.md.answer: The author is considering experimenting with a "Links From Our Sponsor" section next year, after being approached by an advertiser.

supercloudbusting.md.question: How does Dave Vellante define a supercloud?
supercloudbusting.md.answer: Dave Vellante defines supercloud as an architecture that taps the underlying services and primitives of hyperscale clouds to deliver additional value above and beyond what is available from the public cloud provider. It delivers capabilities through software, consumed as services, and can run on a single hyperscale cloud or span multiple clouds.

supercloudbusting.md.question: Where did the trend of supercloud originate as a topic of related work?
supercloudbusting.md.answer: The trend of supercloud relates back to work coming out of Cornell University circa 2015-2016.

supercloudbusting.md.question: What regional expansions are noted for GCP, Azure, and AWS in the post?
supercloudbusting.md.answer: GCP is expanding its region coverage to Dallas, TX, USA; Azure is expanding to Doha, Qatar; and AWS is expanding its Local Zones and Outpost availability to Panama.

the-waiting.md.question: What features and developments were rolled out by YouTube to expand its revenue sources beyond just 'running ads'?
the-waiting.md.answer: To generate revenue beyond running ads, YouTube introduced full feature length movies, deep audience analytics, storage for HD content, large screen formats, partner programs for news media, and location-based services options.

the-waiting.md.question: Why are analyst predictions about Internet titans inaccurate when they use enterprise or carrier assumptions?
the-waiting.md.answer: These predictions are inaccurate because Internet titans do not fit traditional enterprise or carrier patterns; they have unique business goals, multi-use facilities, novel location selections, and vibrant refresh requirements at scale.

the-waiting.md.question: What is the recommended approach for analyzing the financials of Internet infrastructure titans instead of reverse engineering their costs?
the-waiting.md.answer: The author recommends sticking with public financial reporting and waiting for revenue numbers to be shared, rather than attempting to reverse engineer 'secret sauce' costs.

tip-of-the-apache-iceberg.md.question: When did Apache Iceberg start and which company donated it to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF)?
tip-of-the-apache-iceberg.md.answer: Apache Iceberg began in 2017 within Netflix and was donated to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) in 2018.

tip-of-the-apache-iceberg.md.question: What can practitioners deploy using the new public GitHub repository for Compute.AI?
tip-of-the-apache-iceberg.md.answer: Practitioners can now launch a containerized deployment of the ComputeAI SQL engine using the public GitHub repository.

tip-of-the-apache-iceberg.md.question: Why was the commercial entity Tabular formed in 2021?
tip-of-the-apache-iceberg.md.answer: Tabular was formed and funded to simplify, secure, and streamline the adoption of Apache Iceberg.

cost-cause.md.question: How many FinOps practitioners and tools were recently surveyed by the FinOps Foundation?
cost-cause.md.answer: The FinOps Foundation canvased 838 FinOps practitioners regarding their most used of 69 different tools.

cost-cause.md.question: Is Azure's Cost Management tool completely free to use?
cost-cause.md.answer: Azure Cost Management is free for Azure use cases, but using it for AWS incurs fees.

cost-cause.md.question: What tools and services does GCP's DIY approach to cloud billing assume a customer will use?
cost-cause.md.answer: GCP's approach assumes putting billing data into BigQuery and using Looker for visualization.

union-of-the-cloud.md.question: According to the 2021 HashiCorp survey data mentioned in the post, what percentage of companies with annual cloud budgets of $5 million or more operated a Cloud Center of Excellence (CCoE)?
union-of-the-cloud.md.answer: Almost 2/3 (or approximately 66%) of companies with annual cloud budgets of $5 million or more operated a CCoE, compared to only 40% of all respondents.

union-of-the-cloud.md.question: What term does the 2022 HashiCorp and Forrester Consulting study use to refer to centralized cloud functions like a Cloud Center of Excellence (CCoE)?
union-of-the-cloud.md.answer: The study refers to these centralized cloud functions as "platform teams."

union-of-the-cloud.md.question: According to HashiCorp and Forrester Consulting, what is the main problem facing multicloud adoption?
union-of-the-cloud.md.answer: The main problem is not the technology itself, but rather the people—specifically, the lack of people available to staff "cloud platform teams" and "Cloud Centers of Excellence (CCoE)" teams.

the-end-of-the-inflated-expectations.md.question: What is the main thesis regarding the training of A.I. models discussed in the post?
the-end-of-the-inflated-expectations.md.answer: The thesis is that training A.I. models on unfiltered Internet content and unvetted contributor input may be cost-effective, but it will not always provide explicitly intended results that are ready for Enterprise use cases.

the-end-of-the-inflated-expectations.md.question: Which A.I. or M.L. concepts are listed under the 'Peak of Inflated Expectations' from the 2023 Gartner Hype Cycle?
the-end-of-the-inflated-expectations.md.answer: The concepts listed are A.I. TRiSM (Trust, Risk, Security Management), Generative A.I., and A.I. Augmented.

the-end-of-the-inflated-expectations.md.question: What examples are provided in the 'Shot' and 'Chaser' section to illustrate the risks of training A.I. on unvetted content?
the-end-of-the-inflated-expectations.md.answer: The 'Shot' example is from 2016, when Twitter taught Microsoft’s AI chatbot to be racist in less than a day. The 'Chaser' example is from 2023, when Amazon's Alexa claimed that the 2020 election was stolen, citing sources from Rumble and Substack.

opportunities.md.question: What new newsletter features from Buttondown is the author testing in this post?
opportunities.md.answer: The author is testing a Spotify card from the Fudge Sunday playlist and a long-running survey at the end of each email.

opportunities.md.question: Which MLOps (Machine Learning Operations) company is congratulated for receiving new funding?
opportunities.md.answer: Striveworks is congratulated on their new funding.

opportunities.md.question: Which company mentioned in 'Do You Need the Service Catalog' recently received a new round of funding?
opportunities.md.answer: Cortex recently received a new round of funding.

are-you-devx-experienced.md.question: How does the author distinguish between the terms Dev, DevEx, DevX, and DX?
are-you-devx-experienced.md.answer: According to the author, 'Dev' is short for developer or development; 'DevEx' refers to Developer Exchange (associated with activities like Roblox DevEx); 'DevX' is used for Developer Experience to avoid typing two words; and 'DX' refers specifically to the company named DX.

are-you-devx-experienced.md.question: How did Etsy's transition to Developer Experience (DevX) progress over time?
are-you-devx-experienced.md.answer: Etsy's progression to DevX began with basic staff augmentation to handle tasks impacting developer satisfaction and productivity. This effort eventually led to the creation of a formal platform team and an enduring C-level initiative.

are-you-devx-experienced.md.question: According to the author, what two principles are required to make the next industrial revolution possible?
are-you-devx-experienced.md.answer: The author states that the next industrial revolution will only be possible through the Ruthless Removal of Annoyances (RRoA) and the Relentless Pursuit of Automation (RPoA).

van-halen-iso-9000.md.question: What will the Internet of Things (IoT) become as parsing software pervades everyday life?
van-halen-iso-9000.md.answer: According to the post, the Internet of Things will fast become the "Internet of Verified Ordering of Things."

van-halen-iso-9000.md.question: What examples of future technology capabilities are mentioned in the text?
van-halen-iso-9000.md.answer: The author imagines a Siri that can understand legalese, Google Goggles that can scan contracts and advise you not to sign, and a car dashboard that tells you exactly what is wrong when you step into the vehicle.

van-halen-iso-9000.md.question: How have personal firewalls evolved since they were first introduced in the late 90s?
van-halen-iso-9000.md.answer: Initially, early personal firewalls (like Zone Alarm) produced superfluous warnings and alerts that did not necessarily require review. Over time, they underwent hardening and refinement, and today they work seamlessly to provide a more understandable risk impact assessment without requiring constant attention.

generative-ai-thru-this.md.question: What new acronym does the author suggest adding to the online lexicon, and what does it stand for?
generative-ai-thru-this.md.answer: The author suggests adding the acronym "IANALLM", which stands for "I am not a large language model".

generative-ai-thru-this.md.question: According to the author, what is the key to achieving exhaustive productivity in platform engineering?
generative-ai-thru-this.md.answer: The key is the inclusion of generative AI that augments in a human + machine partnership.

generative-ai-thru-this.md.question: What prediction does the author make regarding domain-specific languages (DSLs) and prompt engineers?
generative-ai-thru-this.md.answer: The author predicts there will be progressions in DSLs, leading to the rise of "DSL poets" (prompt engineers) who will flourish and write the essential abstractions for what was once considered business-as-usual IT.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-03-11.md.question: What is the promised speed and valuation of the photonics chiplets mentioned in the post?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-03-11.md.answer: The photonics chiplets promise a speed of 14.4Tbps and have a valuation of $2.5B.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-03-11.md.question: What valuation do the trusted 'Tupperware' software solutions command?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-03-11.md.answer: The trusted 'Tupperware' software solutions command a valuation of $3.5B.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-03-11.md.question: On what day of the week did these two stories catch the author's eye?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-03-11.md.answer: The two stories caught the author's eye on Tuesday.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-10-07.md.question: What does FurtherAI do and how much funding did it receive?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-10-07.md.answer: FurtherAI automates insurance tasks using AI and received $25M in funding.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-10-07.md.question: Which company raised $150M and what is its target market?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-10-07.md.answer: EvenUp raised $150M, and it provides AI tools for law firms.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-10-07.md.question: What is Bee Maps and how much investment did it secure?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-10-07.md.answer: Bee Maps is a decentralized AI dashcam mapping project that secured $32M in funding.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-09-23.md.question: Which humanoid robotics company is currently seeking up to $1B in funding?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-09-23.md.answer: 1X is the humanoid robotics company seeking up to $1B in funding.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-09-23.md.question: How much funding did Distyl AI receive, and what do they specialize in?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-09-23.md.answer: Distyl AI received $175M in funding, and they specialize in AI for business processes.

hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-09-23.md.question: What company received $140M in funding for power management chips?
hot-takes-tuesday-for-2025-09-23.md.answer: Empower Semiconductor received $140M in funding for power management chips.

throwback-thursday-for-2024-12-05.md.question: When was Google NotebookLM first launched?
throwback-thursday-for-2024-12-05.md.answer: Google NotebookLM was launched two years ago.

throwback-thursday-for-2024-12-05.md.question: What was the author's reaction to trying the recent updates of Google NotebookLM a few months ago?
throwback-thursday-for-2024-12-05.md.answer: The author tried the updates a few months ago and thought they were "pretty cool stuff!".

throwback-thursday-for-2024-12-05.md.question: What is happening with the Google NotebookLM team exactly two years after its launch?
throwback-thursday-for-2024-12-05.md.answer: The Google NotebookLM team is undergoing their own updates exactly two years after the launch.

fudge-sunday-once-in-a-pipeline.md.question: Who coined the term "Technical Debt" and when was it coined?
fudge-sunday-once-in-a-pipeline.md.answer: The term "Technical Debt" was coined by Ward Cunningham approximately 30 years ago.

fudge-sunday-once-in-a-pipeline.md.question: What is the difference between the Showback, Chargeback, and Shameback stages of policy progression?
fudge-sunday-once-in-a-pipeline.md.answer: Showback (Crawl) shows what was consumed and who consumed it. Chargeback (Walk) shows who consumed what and draws against the appropriate budget. Shameback (Run) shows who consumed what, draws against the appropriate budget, and reports on the value return (revenue) per investment (consumption costs).

fudge-sunday-once-in-a-pipeline.md.question: What is the purpose of the Infracost tool?
fudge-sunday-once-in-a-pipeline.md.answer: Infracost is a tool that provides cloud cost estimates for Terraform in pull requests and in the CI before the CD, allowing organizations to see estimated costs before they actually incur them.

cant-buy-me-lead-time.md.question: How does the author of the blog post define "lead time" in the context of software development?
cant-buy-me-lead-time.md.answer: The author defines lead time as the measurement of the time between when a developer contributes to the product experience until an end user of that product experiences a desirable impact from the developer's contribution.

cant-buy-me-lead-time.md.question: Can lead time be shortened by reducing quality or bypassing security?
cant-buy-me-lead-time.md.answer: No. The author emphasizes that obtaining a shorter lead time must not be the result of lowering quality, delivering incomplete work, or bypassing security considerations, as quality assurance and safety/security remain critical.

cant-buy-me-lead-time.md.question: What path does the author suggest DevOps pursuits should take for long-term development?
cant-buy-me-lead-time.md.answer: The author suggests that DevOps pursuits should holistically be DevSecOps pursuits (rooted in secure software development practices, quality engineering, and automation-first thinking) and, in the long term, transition into platform engineering pursuits on a path to expressing a more product-led growth culture.

the-5-ps-of-workloads.md.question: What does the author mean by the term "Cloud Computing Canard"?
the-5-ps-of-workloads.md.answer: The author uses "Cloud Computing Canard" to describe the vast oversimplification of IT workload diversity, specifically referring to the rigid "pets vs. cattle" duality that incorrectly suggests there are only two categories of workloads.

the-5-ps-of-workloads.md.question: What are the "5 P's of Workloads" proposed by the author?
the-5-ps-of-workloads.md.answer: The 5 P's of Workloads are a tiered classification system used to rationalize applications within an IT portfolio. They consist of Parrots (long-lived legacy workloads), Parakeets (frequently replaced tactical legacy workloads), Poultry (highly integrated, uniform deployments), Popeyes (orchestrated, self-service workloads or 'Poultry as a Service'), and Pigeons (failed, overly complex and sprawling implementations).

the-5-ps-of-workloads.md.question: How does the author define a "Pigeon" workload?
the-5-ps-of-workloads.md.answer: A Pigeon workload is a failed IT execution where an attempt to create a new delivery method results in an overly complex, sprawling, and cumbersome implementation. It becomes a nuisance that multiplies, requiring maintenance from those who feed it while everyone else forgets why the workload mattered or who owns it.

a-decade-of-github.md.question: What was the author's first GitHub repository?
a-decade-of-github.md.answer: The author's first GitHub repository was a simple XML-based web gadget that mapped traffic cameras for the ATL and RDU areas, originally created as a Google Gadget for iGoogle.

a-decade-of-github.md.question: What tools did the author use to manage and archive their Twitter content before deleting it?
a-decade-of-github.md.answer: The author used tweetmerge to combine multiple Twitter handles, Parse-Twitter-Archive to search older tweets, expired-tweets to check for link rot, twitter-archive-analysis to understand engagement, and twitter-export-image-fill to save rich media.

a-decade-of-github.md.question: Why does the author bookmark and star terminal-based tools like pgcli and ptpython?
a-decade-of-github.md.answer: The author saves these tools to learn more about late-stage LAMP options and to maintain a native terminal experience without having to spawn a desktop IDE.

the-fudge-faq.md.question: Why did the author choose the nickname "fudge"?
the-fudge-faq.md.answer: The nickname started during the days of MUDs and chatters on MegaDiner. The author, who is an engineer, chose "fudge" because most good engineering involves some sort of "fudge" factor.

the-fudge-faq.md.question: Where does the name "qthrul" come from?
the-fudge-faq.md.answer: The name "qthrul" (pronounced Q - Thrul) comes from the numerous mispronunciations and bastardizations of the author's uncommon last name, Cooterell.

the-fudge-faq.md.question: What are the author's ICQ contact numbers?
the-fudge-faq.md.answer: The author's ICQ number is 1351835 for work and 4663668 for home.

miscellaneous-may.md.question: Why does the author consider LinkedIn Newsletters to be a silo?
miscellaneous-may.md.answer: The author views LinkedIn Newsletters as a silo because, despite being able to see their followers, they cannot export them to a new platform without using automation that potentially violates LinkedIn's terms of service.

miscellaneous-may.md.question: What is the author's "hot take" regarding cloud service providers (CSPs) and AI chips?
miscellaneous-may.md.answer: The author's hot take is that cloud service providers will continue to offer NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD chips while also moving forward with their own proprietary chips, which will eventually make their way from the cloud to the edge and the core.

miscellaneous-may.md.question: What does the V.O.W.E.L. acronym stand for, and what is its purpose?
miscellaneous-may.md.answer: V.O.W.E.L. stands for Variety, Opinions, Women, Ethnicities, and Location. Its purpose is to encourage balanced, diverse, and inclusive speaker lineups at events, ensuring a representative range of backgrounds and perspectives.

thinking-out-loud.md.question: What is the "Adaptability Paradox" discussed in the blog post?
thinking-out-loud.md.answer: The blog post describes Kris Krüg's "Adaptability Paradox" as the tension between the need for change and the quest for a stable identity. It questions how one can change with the times without losing sight of who they are, or keep their profile updated without forgetting the "password to their own soul."

thinking-out-loud.md.question: What kind of "avatars" does the author reflect on having?
thinking-out-loud.md.answer: The author reflects on having various avatars throughout their life, including an online writer avatar, a telco ISP consultant nerd avatar from the late 1990s and 2000s, and a datacenter cloud consultant nerd avatar from the 2010s. They also speculate about potential past avatars like an engineering student, lab grunt, busboy, or retail assistant.

thinking-out-loud.md.question: How does the author discover new books for their reading wish list?
thinking-out-loud.md.answer: The author discovers much of their reading by not trying to live every moment online, particularly from events like SXSW where they hear about books from people in the real world. They maintain a Thriftbooks wish list for these discoveries.

pipes-before-pipelines.md.question: What was ReadWriteHack, and what was the author's role?
pipes-before-pipelines.md.answer: ReadWriteHack was a channel on ReadWriteWeb (now ReadWrite) where the author would read about new tools or concepts and then write short "hacks" with links to try them out. Readers were encouraged to participate by reading, writing, and "hacking" around.

pipes-before-pipelines.md.question: What was Yahoo Pipes, and what was its significance for future technology?
pipes-before-pipelines.md.answer: Yahoo Pipes was a tool that the author wrote about in late 2010, which eventually went offline in late 2015. It was significant because it "glimpsed into a lowcode nocode future of user friendlier integration platform as a service (iPaaS)" and outlined a technique now taken for granted using tools like Grammarly or Eleventy's Inclusive Language Plugin.

pipes-before-pipelines.md.question: How are modern data pipelines, such as in Google Maps, leveraging A.I. today and in the future?
pipes-before-pipelines.md.answer: Modern Google Maps experiences increasingly leverage A.I. to provide a preview of directions, exemplified by its "Immersive View" feature. A.I. claims are integrated into product marketing and IT processes for data enrichment, with a promise of future "copilots" that move beyond simple autocomplete, such as for tasks in Microsoft Excel.

memo-to-the-cloud.md.question: What is the primary criticism the author has about SaaS and cloud customer support?
memo-to-the-cloud.md.answer: The author's primary criticism is directed at 'boilerplate responses' in customer support, which they describe as inexcusable, indicating a lack of care, and screaming 'NOT MY PROBLEM.'

memo-to-the-cloud.md.question: How do customers communicate their experiences with SaaS and cloud providers, particularly regarding poor support?
memo-to-the-cloud.md.answer: Customers discuss their experiences, especially concerning boilerplate responses, at user groups, conferences, and local watering holes where they talk shop. The author notes that customers have become an audience that passes notes, whispers, and has conversations about providers.

memo-to-the-cloud.md.question: What specific poor practices should SaaS and cloud providers avoid in their support processes?
memo-to-the-cloud.md.answer: Providers should avoid boilerplate responses, ignoring customer contributions, obfuscating support resolution paths (such as hiding phone numbers, using no-reply emails, or relying on heavy automation for edge cases), and failing to provide regular communication regarding changes for contact and support information (like altering support site navigation or not testing IVR from simulated time zones).

on-metrics.md.question: What is the author's primary concern regarding "marketing intelligence" services?
on-metrics.md.answer: The author questions the general notion of tracking service utilization in the fast-paced world of iterative development with anything but the very broadest and most fluffy of metrics, especially when dealing with a constantly changing landscape.

on-metrics.md.question: Why does the author describe tracked online properties as "moving targets"?
on-metrics.md.answer: The author considers properties like Google+ as "moving targets" because they evolve through new signatures, A/B testing, deep personalization, mobile or closed loop networks, leverage of partners, or shifts in delivery infrastructure to favor SSL-enabled experiences.

on-metrics.md.question: According to the post, how does user privacy and data ownership impact third-party tracking services?
on-metrics.md.answer: The quality of time spent and sharing gestures (text updates vs. photos vs. movies) become harder to glean as this data is considered part of the privacy contract and the salable asset of the property, with the user often being "what is for sale" if the product is free.

esteem-is-stem-plus-ethics-plus-empathy..question: What does STEM stand for, according to the blog post?
esteem-is-stem-plus-ethics-plus-empathy..answer: STEM is the backronym associated with educational pursuits in the areas of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics.

esteem-is-stem-plus-ethics-plus-empathy..question: What new backronym does the author propose and what does it stand for?
esteem-is-stem-plus-ethics-plus-empathy..answer: The author proposes ESTEEM, which stands for Ethics, Science, Technology, Engineering, Empathy, and Mathematics.

esteem-is-stem-plus-ethics-plus-empathy..question: Based on the author's college experience, what is the difference between science and technology?
esteem-is-stem-plus-ethics-plus-empathy..answer: The author learned that science is that which explains and predicts, whereas technology is the response to a perceived need.

tel-aviv-traceroute.md.question: Where is the author currently located?
tel-aviv-traceroute.md.answer: The author is currently in Tel Aviv.

tel-aviv-traceroute.md.question: What issues did the author encounter with their flight?
tel-aviv-traceroute.md.answer: The author's connecting flights were late, and they were told at the ticketing counter that the flight was overbooked, and their seat was given away because they were late to check in.

tel-aviv-traceroute.md.question: Why did the author use an old camera instead of their Nikon?
tel-aviv-traceroute.md.answer: The author used an old camera because they couldn’t bear losing the Nikon to a random mishap.

emerging-hype.md.question: What was the top blog post topic identified by searching Gartner blogs using keywords from the 2020 Hype Cycle for Emerging Technologies?
emerging-hype.md.answer: "Social Distancing Technologies" enjoyed the top topic spot, matching the Peak of Inflated Blog Post Themes.

emerging-hype.md.question: When did blogging become a formal content channel for Gartner?
emerging-hype.md.answer: Blogging at Gartner has been a formal content channel since late 2008.

emerging-hype.md.question: Which topic is predicted to be a significant focus for future blog posts?
emerging-hype.md.answer: The "Digital Twin" topic is predicted to be significant, as it enjoyed a high number of blog posts and is considered broad enough to sweep in many more stories and blog posts in the coming years.

be-my-advisor.md.question: What is the author's general approach to advisor invitations?
be-my-advisor.md.answer: The author finds advisor invitations flattering, exciting, and tempting, but also considers them a huge commitment. They state that they say 'no' more often than 'yes' and take the time to explain their reasons for declining.

be-my-advisor.md.question: What are the typical terms when the author agrees to be a startup advisor?
be-my-advisor.md.answer: When agreeing to be an advisor, the author purposely limits their grant size to 0.1% to 0.75% over a 24 to 36-month period of monthly vesting without a cliff. The goal is to be useful and timely while preserving equity for the team, and they often require an expense clause consistent with their own consulting rate card.

be-my-advisor.md.question: What factors influence the author's decision to decline an advisor role?
be-my-advisor.md.answer: The author declines advisor roles when the commitment or proposed terms don't align with their value proposition. They consider their hypothetical value (e.g., $100 to $250 an hour) and question if a startup's offer of a large grant (e.g., 1%) for limited hours (e.g., 24-30 hours per month) represents an efficient use of resources, suggesting that the equivalent 'spent' amount (~$200k over 24 months) might be better directed towards an angel investment or a full-time team member. They also note their risk increases as grant size increases.

quantum-leap-years.md.question: How has public interest in quantum computing evolved over time?
quantum-leap-years.md.answer: The topic of quantum computing has steadily moved towards the realm of popular science, with recognition on the rise. While Google Trends indicate that interest has risen and fallen, there has been a steady uptick in searches. In just over 15 years, the interest has risen to the level of demand for summaries targeting CIOs.

quantum-leap-years.md.question: Where was quantum computing positioned on Gartner's Hype Cycle around 2000?
quantum-leap-years.md.answer: Almost 20 years ago, Gartner placed quantum computing on their hype cycle. On the Gartner Hype Cycle for 2000, quantum computing was situated on the far far left, in contrast to many other entries that are now commonplace.

quantum-leap-years.md.question: What are the distinct strategic goals for quantum computing in the US and Europe?
quantum-leap-years.md.answer: In the US, the race to be a leader in quantum computing solidified with the National Quantum Initiative Act being signed into law and AWS telegraphing potential plans. In Europe, initiatives aim to harness quantum computing for purposes that will challenge nation state surveillance actors and the platform implications for global consumer advertising juggernauts, with potential far-reaching implications for privacy and B2C aggregation.

roll-your-own.md.question: What trend has the author noticed regarding content hosting?
roll-your-own.md.answer: The author has noticed a renewed interest in the repatriation of content from centralized social platform juggernauts to more self-hosted options.

roll-your-own.md.question: What event is highlighted as a significant turning point for social media scrutiny?
roll-your-own.md.answer: March 2018 is highlighted as the period when social media finally saw the culmination of unflattering coverage by both tech press and traditional media, followed by users questioning if there were alternatives.

roll-your-own.md.question: What types of alternatives to large centralized social media platforms are discussed?
roll-your-own.md.answer: The post discusses alternatives ranging from smaller centralized social platforms to more interesting approaches like self-hosted options with decentralized, federated, and distributed sensibilities. Examples provided include the Diaspora* Project, Mastodon, Movim, and Aether.

the-perfect-team.md.question: What is the definition of The Perfect Team?
the-perfect-team.md.answer: The Perfect Team is defined as one person to do it, one person to write it down, and one person to think ahead.

the-perfect-team.md.question: What is the design concept behind The Perfect Team?
the-perfect-team.md.answer: The Perfect Team is designed as a minimum viable concept applied to innovation centers or ad hoc teaming.

the-perfect-team.md.question: Why is emphasizing only the 'person to do it' considered a folly?
the-perfect-team.md.answer: Emphasizing only the 'person to do it' limits visibility and awareness for the person to write it down and forgoes cycles of thought for the person to think ahead. This 'do it only' approach is a common folly of so-called heroic culture companies that are often regarded as inconsistent in execution capability.

cadillac-webassembly-line.md.question: What is WebAssembly (Wasm) and what is an example of its capability mentioned in the post?
cadillac-webassembly-line.md.answer: WebAssembly (Wasm) refers to ubiquitous stack-based virtual machines. An example of its capability is being able to run a late 1990s classic game like Diablo in a Safari browser, even from a low-end Linux or Apple laptop.

cadillac-webassembly-line.md.question: Who is identified as a central figure in driving the web industry towards ubiquitous stack-based virtual machines, and what was their vision?
cadillac-webassembly-line.md.answer: Sundar Pichai, now the CEO of Alphabet, is identified as that central figure. Ten years ago, as an executive at Google, he envisioned a future where a web browser would be sufficient to run any application anywhere on very low-cost computing devices.

cadillac-webassembly-line.md.question: What speculative use cases for WebAssembly were discussed around 2016?
cadillac-webassembly-line.md.answer: Around 2016, speculative use cases for WebAssembly were categorized into computationally expensive number crunching (like games, media, and data) and server-side pre-processing of what was previously client-side processing.

genai-june.md.question: What are the common suggestions from readers regarding free and paid newsletters?
genai-june.md.answer: For free newsletters, common suggestions include sponsorships offsetting operating costs, and weekly digests driving paid subscriptions, with daily formats reserved for paid subscriptions. For paid newsletters, they must have clear value and daily delivery, include comments and author replies, and offer paid options like Stripe, Paypal, and checks by mail.

genai-june.md.question: What is the author's view on GenAI based on the post?
genai-june.md.answer: The author notes that GenAI has been treated like a 'punching bag' in the popular press and observes a recent ramp-up in GenAI references. While the author has experienced real benefits from GenAI, they also state that GenAI isn't magic and all-knowing yet, and that the hype around it is not a new phenomenon.

genai-june.md.question: What new newsletter initiatives are mentioned in the blog post?
genai-june.md.answer: The blog post announces that the Fudge Sunday Weekly newsletter is now sponsored, and its archive is also open for sponsorship by topics. Furthermore, the author is preparing to launch a new daily paid newsletter subscription model called Hot Fudge Daily.

ai-for-you.md.question: What was the growth rate of A.I. implementation in enterprises between 2015 and 2019?
ai-for-you.md.answer: According to Gartner, there was almost 300% growth in the percentage of organizations that had implemented some form of A.I. from 2015-2019.

ai-for-you.md.question: Why are Independent Software Vendors (ISVs) integrating A.I. into their offerings?
ai-for-you.md.answer: Independent software vendors (ISVs) are integrating A.I. to maintain relevance and increase value year over year, influenced by a mix of FOMO and customer expectations for value stream mapping.

ai-for-you.md.question: Which four Enterprise ISVs are discussed as examples of companies acquiring M.L. and A.I. related businesses?
ai-for-you.md.answer: The blog post highlights Atlassian, ServiceNow, Workday, and Salesforce as Enterprise ISVs with the financial capacity to acquire M.L. and A.I. related companies.

zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2023.md.question: Is the Fudge Sunday newsletter free, and does it contain ads or tracking?
zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2023.md.answer: Yes, the Fudge Sunday newsletter remains a free newsletter. The author states there are no referral links or interstitial banner ads, and all forms of tracking (such as Buttondown click tracking, Google Analytics, and LinkedIn Creator Analytics) have been removed.

zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2023.md.question: What is the author's primary workflow for creating and delivering the Fudge Sunday newsletter?
zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2023.md.answer: The author's 10-step process involves using Obsidian for daily notes, Microsoft Visual Studio Code for editing, and Eleventy (specifically Eleventy Excellent) to generate the newsletter from markdown files. After committing to GitHub and an automated build on Netlify, Buttondown's RSS to Email delivers the content, which is then manually replicated to LinkedIn Newsletter. The process concludes with monitoring for replies and automated GitHub Actions.

zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2023.md.question: How does the author find content and ideas for the Fudge Sunday newsletter?
zettelkasten-end-of-year-for-2023.md.answer: The author gathers content by reading approximately 100 newsletters (after pruning Substack), uses Obsidian for notes, and manages a tamed Safari Reading List. For search, the author uses DuckDuckGo, plans to experiment with Kagi Search, and occasionally uses Google Search and Bing Search with anonymization. The author has also stopped using Twitter/X.

fractional-futures.md.question: What is a fractional executive role?
fractional-futures.md.answer: A fractional executive role is ideal for companies needing strategic executive support without the overhead of a full-time hire. The concept is not new and likely dates back at least 20 years.

fractional-futures.md.question: What events is the author planning to attend or participate in soon?
fractional-futures.md.answer: The author is headed to Red Hat Summit and AnsibleFest in Denver, Dell Technologies World in Las Vegas, and will be a Tech Field Day delegate at Qlik Connect in Orlando.

fractional-futures.md.question: Are there any communities or resources mentioned for fractional executives?
fractional-futures.md.answer: Yes, the post directs readers to 'Fractionals United' and 'Business Talent Group.' It also mentions articles from SDxCentral and HR Brew, and refers to the 'Fractional Defined' landing page from Fractionals United for definitions.

out-of-sequence-update.md.question: What tool was used to craft the content of this newsletter?
out-of-sequence-update.md.answer: Obsidian was used to craft the content of this newsletter.

out-of-sequence-update.md.question: How was the jump from an Obsidian note to a Buttondown draft email accomplished?
out-of-sequence-update.md.answer: The jump from an Obsidian note to a Buttondown draft email was accomplished via [obsidian-buttondown](https://github.com/caro401/obsidian-buttondown).

out-of-sequence-update.md.question: What are the benefits of this content creation workflow?
out-of-sequence-update.md.answer: Benefits of this workflow include staying in markdown, updating ideas from any device with Obsidian installed, syncing to GitHub as needed or to Buttondown, and being able to decide on Netlify (web) first or Buttondown (email) first.

run-fly-visualize.md.question: What were the two major Qlik product-related themes highlighted at Qlik Connect 2024?
run-fly-visualize.md.answer: The two major Qlik product-related themes picked up at Qlik Connect 2024 were the importance of writeback and visualizations to Qlik Connect attendees.

run-fly-visualize.md.question: What are Qlik's three distinct coding approaches for embedded analytics?
run-fly-visualize.md.answer: Qlik refers to three distinct coding approaches for embedded analytics: PRO-code, LOW-code, and NO-code. PRO-code reflects leading-edge visualization possibilities, often for power users or partners. LOW-code leverages nebula.js for faster results. NO-code democratizes access to pre-built visualizations without requiring coding from the end user.

run-fly-visualize.md.question: How does the author believe the Qlik Partner ecosystem will evolve?
run-fly-visualize.md.answer: The author believes Qlik Partners will execute one or more strategies: Embrace (e.g., PRO-code differentiation or LOW-code matriculation), Expand (e.g., Qlik training services for LOW-code/NO-code or professional services for cloud transition), and Diverge (e.g., embracing other MQ players or deeper UX design patterns).

reading-fudge-sunday-test-1.md.question: What is the author currently doing and what podcast setup are they using?
reading-fudge-sunday-test-1.md.answer: The author is currently reading the latest issue of Fudge Sunday and using the microphone array from their MacBook Air to publish directly via in-browser podcast recording on Substack.

reading-fudge-sunday-test-1.md.question: What equipment does the author plan to use for podcasting next time?
reading-fudge-sunday-test-1.md.answer: Next time, the author plans to use a Shure SM57 microphone, a PreSonus Studio 24c, and GarageBand software.

reading-fudge-sunday-test-1.md.question: How will the author publish their podcast next time?
reading-fudge-sunday-test-1.md.answer: Next time, the author will upload the rendered audio file to the podcast publishing page on Substack.

software-is-a-villain.md.question: What metaphor does the post use to describe the impact of software on the world?
software-is-a-villain.md.answer: The post describes software as a Galactus-style entity that consumes entire planets as a snack.

software-is-a-villain.md.question: According to the post, what does culture do to strategy?
software-is-a-villain.md.answer: Culture eats strategy for breakfast, employing caloric restriction and ketogenic diet principles to ensure it has great abs.

software-is-a-villain.md.question: What is the author's stance on the attribution of the quote about culture and strategy?
software-is-a-villain.md.answer: The author notes that it is difficult to find a reliable citation for the quote, as it is often circularly referenced and incorrectly attributed to Peter Drucker.

tech-journalism.md.question: What is the primary purpose of the suggested 'Tech Blog Free Diet'?
tech-journalism.md.answer: The diet is intended to act as a cleansing process to remove the 'impurities' that build up from regularly consuming tech blog coverage, serving as an A/B test to see how you feel after avoiding certain types of content.

tech-journalism.md.question: What are some of the conventions that indicate a tech blog should be avoided according to the author?
tech-journalism.md.answer: The author recommends disconnecting from tech blogs that use horse race metaphors, physical violence or war references, hyperbole, all internal links, consistent rivalry references, blatant refusal to use proper terminology, catering to irrational fears, or meta-commentary about the act of writing.

tech-journalism.md.question: What specific activity is recommended for Friday during the Tech Blog Free Diet?
tech-journalism.md.answer: On Friday, the author suggests finding your favorite project, identifying the top 10 committers, and sending a personal thank you note to the people ranked 7th, 8th, 9th, and 10th if they can be found online easily.

burl-ives-workloads.md.question: Why does the author prefer hosting content on their own website rather than on platforms like Medium or Branch?
burl-ives-workloads.md.answer: The author views platforms like Medium and Branch as non-durable places for content. They observed that personal blog posts receive significantly more views than content posted on Medium, and they are concerned about content loss on platforms like Branch, which they describe as fragile.

burl-ives-workloads.md.question: What are 'Burl Ives' workloads?
burl-ives-workloads.md.answer: 'Burl Ives' workloads are those that will not migrate to the cloud due to specific requirements such as latency, isolation, legal or licensing constraints, cultural factors, or purposeful inattention by independent software vendors (ISVs) and highly customized design choices.

burl-ives-workloads.md.question: Do 'Burl Ives' workloads preclude the use of generalized x86-based cloud infrastructure?
burl-ives-workloads.md.answer: No, it does not preclude placement within an ultra-generalized x86-based cloud, but it raises questions about how effectively the workload runs when measured against the specific concerns that keep it from moving to the cloud.

you-get-a-line-and-ill-get-a-poll-result.question: How should organizations decide on the placement of workloads?
you-get-a-line-and-ill-get-a-poll-result.answer: Placement decisions should be data-driven throughout the workload lifecycle and across the workload landscape, using math to govern the edge to core to public cloud conversation.

you-get-a-line-and-ill-get-a-poll-result.question: What is the outlook for edge computing according to the post?
you-get-a-line-and-ill-get-a-poll-result.answer: Expect to see more edge use cases emerging in smaller spaces and a broader variety of locations as the concept of the 'edge use case' becomes standard practice.

you-get-a-line-and-ill-get-a-poll-result.question: What is recommended regarding cloud cost optimization?
you-get-a-line-and-ill-get-a-poll-result.answer: Cloud should be viewed as an operating model rather than a place. While some argue for disciplined repatriation to save costs, it is advised to perform the necessary math before making such decisions.

are-you-gonna-go-parquet.md.question: What was the primary focus of Apache Parquet when it was first released over ten years ago?
are-you-gonna-go-parquet.md.answer: When Apache Parquet was released over ten years ago, it was designed as a more efficient file format with a specific emphasis on the speed to read data.

are-you-gonna-go-parquet.md.question: How has the focus of data analytics shifted in recent years compared to the early days of 'big data'?
are-you-gonna-go-parquet.md.answer: While the early focus was on big data within the Apache Hadoop ecosystem, the current market emphasis has shifted toward 'speed to value' to support the needs of A.I. and M.L. models.

are-you-gonna-go-parquet.md.question: Does the blog post mention Databricks or Snowflake as part of the 'big data' companies from 2013?
are-you-gonna-go-parquet.md.answer: No. The author notes that Databricks took its first funding in 2013 and Snowflake did not launch until 2014, so they were not part of the 2013 big data landscape described.

dont-worry-bout-sre.md.question: How does Google define Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)?
dont-worry-bout-sre.md.answer: According to Google, SRE is what you get when you treat operations as if it is a software problem.

dont-worry-bout-sre.md.question: When is human intervention typically required for cloud support?
dont-worry-bout-sre.md.answer: Human intervention is typically required when a support case or ticket is successfully opened after meeting necessary triage requirements. It is also required more frequently when automation adoption is low and relies on subjective, qualitative observation or individual decision-making.

dont-worry-bout-sre.md.question: Is it realistic to worry that a platform like Twitter will go down without SRE support?
dont-worry-bout-sre.md.answer: It is reasonable to take precautions for any platform feature that assumes a long-term reduction in SRE support. While massive outages are less likely than they were in the past, risks include the inability to pay bills, staff turnover, and a shift in company priorities that may lead to the degradation or deprecation of existing features.

augmented-reality.md.question: What is one potential use for augmented reality in the context of self-driving cars?
augmented-reality.md.answer: The author suggests that AR could be used as an advertising modality, where the human occupant becomes an audience for commercials displayed against the real-world environment while the car drives itself.

augmented-reality.md.question: What concern does the author raise regarding the rapid growth of machine learning?
augmented-reality.md.answer: The author is concerned that the hunger for progress may lead to the use of flawed data sets, which could result in machine learning applications that are unethical, unrealistic, or unsatisfactory.

augmented-reality.md.question: How does the author propose we mitigate the risks associated with testing new technologies?
augmented-reality.md.answer: The author suggests using augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR) as safer constructs to test and apply machine learning findings before unleashing them in the real world.

twit-can-happen.md.question: What potential change to Twitter's service model has been discussed by Elon Musk and David Sacks?
twit-can-happen.md.answer: Elon Musk and David Sacks have discussed the possibility of putting all of Twitter behind a paywall, which might allow users limited free browsing before requiring a subscription.

twit-can-happen.md.question: Why does the author consider link rot a significant issue?
twit-can-happen.md.answer: The author notes that link rot matters for the same reason that domain names are perishable: it leads to content drift, where links can become broken or redirected to unintended content.

twit-can-happen.md.question: What is the author's suggested solution to address issues like link rot and potential paywalls on Twitter?
twit-can-happen.md.answer: The author suggests that archiving content is a solution, proposing that Twitter should make the process of archiving content as easy and robust as possible.

multicloud-march.md.question: What is the author's opinion regarding the current state of multicloud interoperability?
multicloud-march.md.answer: The author believes that achieving a uniformly multicloud experience is currently challenging due to differences in cloud service provider networking features, pricing, and the difficulty of using the same IP range across multiple providers.

multicloud-march.md.question: How does the author characterize cloud egress fees?
multicloud-march.md.answer: The author compares cloud egress fees to the long-distance charges of the 1990s and predicts that they will eventually become completely unjustifiable.

multicloud-march.md.question: Which cloud service provider was the first to offer 100 Gbps connectivity?
multicloud-march.md.answer: According to the post, Microsoft Azure was the first to offer 100 Gbps connectivity, followed by Google Cloud Platform in 2019, and then AWS in 2021.

google-nonplussed.md.question: Why did the author decide to export their Google+ data?
google-nonplussed.md.answer: The author exported their data due to warnings and advice provided in April 2019 regarding the impending closure of Google+ for most users.

google-nonplussed.md.question: What determines whether a Google service will continue to run according to the author?
google-nonplussed.md.answer: The author suggests that services continue to run as long as there is a viable model for monetization; if a service lacks a model or if a better monetization model exists, the service is likely to be shut down.

google-nonplussed.md.question: Why does the author believe Google+ for G-Suite might remain available?
google-nonplussed.md.answer: Because G-Suite is monetized through subscription services, the author believes the Google+ service within G-Suite is more likely to continue as long as there is a sufficiently large number of enterprise customers using it.

a-cold-shot-and-chaser.md.question: What is the 'shot and chaser' format used in the blog post?
a-cold-shot-and-chaser.md.answer: The 'shot and chaser' format is a technique where the author references a prior topic, prediction, or link and compares it to a more recent or current update to see how the subject has progressed.

a-cold-shot-and-chaser.md.question: How does the author describe the progression from data centers to wisdom centers?
a-cold-shot-and-chaser.md.answer: The author defines the progression as: data centers < information centers < knowledge centers < wisdom centers, noting that current technology has primarily focused on data centers rather than the higher-level centers.

a-cold-shot-and-chaser.md.question: What was the status of the author's prediction regarding Helium and FreedomFi?
a-cold-shot-and-chaser.md.answer: The author initially expressed hedged confidence in Helium and FreedomFi in early 2022. While they later highlighted critical feedback regarding Helium's revenue, they also noted that Nova Labs had subsequently acquired FreedomFi.

internet-of-things-have-changed.md.question: Was Nest the first company to produce a WiFi-enabled smart thermostat?
internet-of-things-have-changed.md.answer: No, Nest was not the first. The article notes that Ecobee released a WiFi-enabled smart thermostat in 2009, two years before Nest arrived in 2011.

internet-of-things-have-changed.md.question: When did Google acquire Nest?
internet-of-things-have-changed.md.answer: Google purchased Nest in 2014 for $3.2 billion.

internet-of-things-have-changed.md.question: What advice does the author give regarding home automation investments?
internet-of-things-have-changed.md.answer: The author suggests that home automation should only be pursued if you have money to burn and do not view it as a long-term investment. If you do plan for the long term, the author emphasizes the importance of documenting everything so the operating manual is easy to find and apply for those who follow you.

why-is-the-network-slow.md.question: Why is there a need to decrease latency in modern technology?
why-is-the-network-slow.md.answer: Decreasing latency is essential because it is a critical component of the overall user experience (UX). Since technology serves to address perceived needs, companies invest in reducing latency to improve how users interact with applications, as latency can be a source of frustration and inconsistency.

why-is-the-network-slow.md.question: Did the implementation of SSL security affect website performance?
why-is-the-network-slow.md.answer: Yes, as SSL became more prevalent, its initial implementation made websites and applications appear slower. The industry responded to this latency challenge on multiple fronts to balance the need for security with performance requirements.

why-is-the-network-slow.md.question: How have companies like AWS and Microsoft Azure addressed latency concerns for cloud services?
why-is-the-network-slow.md.answer: These companies have worked to move cloud resources closer to users by building massive datacenters near major population centers. Examples include AWS Local Zones and Microsoft Azure Edge Zones, which help meet the latency requirements for modern applications.

take-the-bot-datadome.md.question: Why do concert tickets often sell out within minutes of going on sale online?
take-the-bot-datadome.md.answer: Tickets sell out quickly because of bots and automation technology used by scalpers to acquire a large number of tickets for the secondary market, rather than for personal use.

take-the-bot-datadome.md.question: What distinguishes DataDome from other anti-bot solution providers mentioned in the text?
take-the-bot-datadome.md.answer: The primary differentiator noted for DataDome is its accessibility, with the company claiming that a digital business can activate its protection in less than one hour.

take-the-bot-datadome.md.question: How do anti-bot companies and the bot-using community interact over time?
take-the-bot-datadome.md.answer: There is an ongoing escalation where anti-bot companies provide protection for storefronts, followed by the scraping or scalping community developing tools to bypass those defenses, leading to a continuous cycle of iteration that increasingly incorporates AI and machine learning.

plants-and-progression.md.question: What is Data Garden?
plants-and-progression.md.answer: Data Garden is a multidisciplinary collective that explores the fusion of nature and technology by using data from plants to create music.

plants-and-progression.md.question: What is the historical significance of the circular patterns seen in rural American farmlands from the air?
plants-and-progression.md.answer: These patterns are not alien crop circles, but rather the result of center-pivot irrigation farm operations used to grow crops and natural fibers.

plants-and-progression.md.question: What call to action does the author suggest for those attending technology industry conferences?
plants-and-progression.md.answer: The author encourages attendees to ask conference planners and organizers if a nature-connected experiential setting will be offered, as this simple act can spark curiosity and lead to change.

nuance-repatriation-and-ipass.md.question: What is the accessibility feature for speech to text called on Apple devices?
nuance-repatriation-and-ipass.md.answer: Apple refers to its speech to text accessibility feature as Dictate or Dictation.

nuance-repatriation-and-ipass.md.question: Who acquired Nuance in 2022?
nuance-repatriation-and-ipass.md.answer: Microsoft completed the acquisition of Nuance in early 2022.

nuance-repatriation-and-ipass.md.question: What is Activepieces?
nuance-repatriation-and-ipass.md.answer: Activepieces is an open source iPaaS (integration platform as a service) project that aims to provide features and functionality similar to tools like Zapier, Tray, MuleSoft, and Boomi.

one-idp-piece-at-a-time.md.question: What is the Internal Developer Platform (IDP) topic trend when compared to DevOps?
one-idp-piece-at-a-time.md.answer: When compared to DevOps, the internal developer platform trend is described as a sobering purple bromide reality.

one-idp-piece-at-a-time.md.question: How many core components of an IDP did the internaldeveloperplatform.org GitHub repository identify?
one-idp-piece-at-a-time.md.answer: The internaldeveloperplatform.org repository identified five core components of an IDP.

one-idp-piece-at-a-time.md.question: Who identified the eight fallacies of an Internal Developer Platform?
one-idp-piece-at-a-time.md.answer: The eight fallacies of an IDP were identified by Ravi Lachhman, who is a Field CTO at Shipa and a Product Manager at Harness.

feeling-so-really-simple-syndication.md.question: What is the primary purpose of RSS and Atom?
feeling-so-really-simple-syndication.md.answer: RSS (RDF Site Summary or Really Simple Syndication) and Atom serve as a way for users to subscribe to articles they want to read, providing an alternative to keeping dozens of browser tabs open.

feeling-so-really-simple-syndication.md.question: What are RSS/Atom feeds commonly associated with in 2022?
feeling-so-really-simple-syndication.md.answer: As of 2022, RSS/Atom feeds are primarily associated with podcasts and newsletters.

map-of-the-platformatique.md.question: Is there currently a dominant, native internal developer platform (IDP) marketplace offered by cloud service providers?
map-of-the-platformatique.md.answer: No, there is currently no dominant native internal developer platform marketplace offered by cloud service providers, though the author suggests this is likely a temporary condition.

map-of-the-platformatique.md.question: What is Wardley Mapping?
map-of-the-platformatique.md.answer: Wardley Mapping is an approach used for visualizing the lifecycle of a market, moving from genesis to commodity.

map-of-the-platformatique.md.question: Which companies does the post mention as examples of SaaS options for internal developer platforms found on some cloud service provider marketplaces?
map-of-the-platformatique.md.answer: The post mentions Cycloid and Humanitec as examples of SaaS options for internal developer platforms, noting that these appear on some cloud service provider marketplaces while others may list none at all.

hanging-out-my-shingle.md.question: How did the author use Generative AI for their farewell email from IBM?
hanging-out-my-shingle.md.answer: The author used a watsonx Assistant with the Granite model to draft the layout of their farewell email, which they then customized with their own personalized flourishes.

hanging-out-my-shingle.md.question: What is the purpose of the Job Search Council (JSC) the author joined?
hanging-out-my-shingle.md.answer: The Job Search Council is a mutual support group that utilizes 'Never Search Alone' techniques to help members find jobs they will love.

hanging-out-my-shingle.md.question: What kind of services is the author offering through cuthrell.consulting?
hanging-out-my-shingle.md.answer: The author is offering fractional strategic roles and advisory services, specifically focusing on growth and pragmatic GenAI and data platforms for startups, emerging technology companies, Telco, Media, Entertainment companies, and Venture Capital/investment firms.

do-you-need-the-service-catalog.md.question: What is a service catalog in the context of developer experience?
do-you-need-the-service-catalog.md.answer: A service catalog is an exhaustively curated, self-service list of developer-empowering capabilities that is easily discovered, accessed, and consumed as a foundational part of developer experience, often supported by an internal developer platform or developer portal.

do-you-need-the-service-catalog.md.question: How does a software team-specific service catalog differ from a traditional IT service catalog?
do-you-need-the-service-catalog.md.answer: While a traditional IT service catalog is typically a portal for end users to request common services like hardware, software licenses, or technical support, a software team-specific service catalog is tailored to the unique needs of application developers, which is increasingly important as businesses embrace digital transformation.

do-you-need-the-service-catalog.md.question: What factors determine the look and feel of a developer portal or service catalog?
do-you-need-the-service-catalog.md.answer: The look and feel of a developer portal or service catalog ultimately depends on the specific needs of the developers it serves and how effectively the platform engineering team presents the curated elements.

life-at-34000-feet.md.question: What was AirFone?
life-at-34000-feet.md.answer: AirFone was an in-flight communication technology introduced in the early 1980s that allowed passengers to make phone calls from airplanes using cordless handsets.

life-at-34000-feet.md.question: Why did in-flight phone services like AirFone eventually end?
life-at-34000-feet.md.answer: The service gave way to market realities, as the allure of making phone calls from airplanes faded and usage declined over time.

life-at-34000-feet.md.question: How has in-flight broadband connectivity evolved since 2011?
life-at-34000-feet.md.answer: While early air-to-ground broadband in 2011 often suffered from degraded performance due to high contention for shared resources, modern in-flight broadband services have improved significantly, with some now providing speeds faster than the Wi-Fi found in coffee shops or hotels.

networks-security-and-an-easier-ev.md.question: What is the format of the 'Shot and Chaser' segments used in the blog?
networks-security-and-an-easier-ev.md.answer: The format involves calling back to prior topics or links and comparing them to a more recent or current link for the week.

networks-security-and-an-easier-ev.md.question: Which company did Cisco recently acquire, as noted in the 'Chaser' section?
networks-security-and-an-easier-ev.md.answer: Cisco acquired Isovalent, a company known for the creation of Cilium Tetragon.

networks-security-and-an-easier-ev.md.question: What two standards does the author suggest keeping an eye on to potentially improve the EV recharging experience?
networks-security-and-an-easier-ev.md.answer: The author identifies the Open Charge Point Protocol (OCPP) version 2.1 and IEC 61850 as two standards to watch for making EV recharging easier.

thinking-remotely.md.question: What are some ways to maintain a social work environment while working remotely?
thinking-remotely.md.answer: You can maintain social interactions by scheduling virtual lunches via tools like Zoom or Lunchclub AI, and by utilizing ChatOps tools such as Slack, Microsoft Teams, or Discord to create dedicated channels for casual conversation and banter similar to an office break room.

thinking-remotely.md.question: How can employees improve their home network performance for work?
thinking-remotely.md.answer: To improve network performance, employees should check if their company offers stipends for home office expenses to purchase a better router, utilize split-tunnel VPNs, switch to SaaS platforms like G Suite or Microsoft Office 365, install a pi-hole for DNS-level ad blocking, and set up a personal dashboard on Down Detector to monitor service statuses.

thinking-remotely.md.question: What techniques does the author suggest for maintaining a work-life balance while working from home?
thinking-remotely.md.answer: The author suggests owning your calendar to protect time for yourself, strictly enforcing hard stops for meetings, using delayed email delivery to respect colleagues' off-hours, and ensuring that you actually take your earned vacation days, even if they are taken as stay-cations.

ready-to-grow.md.question: What is the expected growth trend for the predictive analytics market according to the post?
ready-to-grow.md.answer: Predictive analytics is a fast-growing market that the author suggests could easily approach approximately $1 trillion USD within a decade.

ready-to-grow.md.question: How does the author characterize the use of predictive analytics in online shopping?
ready-to-grow.md.answer: The author identifies the most conspicuous use of predictive analytics in e-commerce as sophisticated shopping experiences that provide real-time advertising based on items other similar customers have purchased.

ready-to-grow.md.question: Why is the author ending their audio podcast testing on Substack?
ready-to-grow.md.answer: The author is ending the podcast tests because they determined that the Substack iOS app already provides the functionality to read the text of a post aloud, making the separate audio recordings superfluous.

security-on-the-agenda.md.question: What common issue did the author observe regarding the placement of security topics on conference agendas?
security-on-the-agenda.md.answer: The author noted that security topics are often placed at the very end of conference agendas, sometimes leading to them being cut short or skipped if previous sessions run over their allotted time.

security-on-the-agenda.md.question: How does the author describe the perception of security within many professional environments?
security-on-the-agenda.md.answer: The author highlights a contradiction where, despite the common mantra that 'security is everyone's job,' it is frequently viewed as a barrier to innovation or secondary to developer productivity.

security-on-the-agenda.md.question: What is the author's observation regarding the inclusion of security topics at the 2024 South by Southwest (SxSW) conference?
security-on-the-agenda.md.answer: The author was pleasantly surprised to find a significant number of programming events at SxSW 2024 that explicitly include the word 'security' in their titles and abstracts, covering diverse areas such as design, democracy, and technology.

me-and-my-posse.md.question: What was the prediction Michael Dell made during the 2017 IQT Day analyst event?
me-and-my-posse.md.answer: Michael Dell predicted that we would soon have IoT processors costing fractions of a cent, which would serve as building blocks for a future digital mirror of the world.

me-and-my-posse.md.question: What does the acronym POSSE stand for in the context of content publishing?
me-and-my-posse.md.answer: POSSE stands for Publish (on your) Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere, which is the practice of posting content on your own site first before sharing copies or links on third-party social media platforms.

me-and-my-posse.md.question: Why did the author decide to move their newsletter from Revue to Substack?
me-and-my-posse.md.answer: The author moved due to concerns regarding a potential 'change in control' at Revue and the need to ensure their content was not sequestered, emphasizing that one should never rely on a third-party service as a primary backup strategy.

thanks-again-in-2023.md.question: Which tools is the author planning to use to tag over 200 posts?
thanks-again-in-2023.md.answer: The author intends to try out spaCy, yake, and KeyBERT for tagging posts before the next newsletter.

thanks-again-in-2023.md.question: What trend has the author observed regarding social media links in the newsletters they read?
thanks-again-in-2023.md.answer: The author has noticed a decline in embedded links to Twitter/X in recent months and has not seen a corresponding rise in links from platforms like Bluesky, Threads, or Mastodon.

thanks-again-in-2023.md.question: Why does the author suggest that Twitter/X links might be appearing less frequently?
thanks-again-in-2023.md.answer: The author suggests it may be due to the difficulty or unreliability of embedding social content, noting that tweets may not always appear as rich media, often forcing users to rely on screenshots instead.

such-tweet-nothing.md.question: What primary factors led to the inception of the IndieWeb movement?
such-tweet-nothing.md.answer: The IndieWeb movement was driven by concerns regarding how social media companies handled user-generated content and the observation of destructive patterns, which were highlighted by a series of site-deaths.

such-tweet-nothing.md.question: Besides Mastodon, what are some other examples of platforms mentioned in the fediverse?
such-tweet-nothing.md.answer: The blog post highlights Akkoma as an interesting fork in fediverse projects, mentions the ongoing development of Bonfire, and notes that the long-lived diaspora project recently celebrated over ten years of community contributions.

such-tweet-nothing.md.question: How does the author plan to change the format of Fudge Sunday issues this year?
such-tweet-nothing.md.answer: The author intends to keep all Fudge Sunday issues to just under 500 words throughout the year.

fuzz-jam-june.md.question: What is the growing importance of fuzzing in platform engineering?
fuzz-jam-june.md.answer: Fuzzing is becoming increasingly important for security and reliability, as evidenced by numerous CNCF projects—including Dapr, Kubernetes Cluster API, and Helm—integrating continuous fuzzing and completing fuzzing security audits.

fuzz-jam-june.md.question: Is it important for fuzzing to be part of the shipped code's toolchain?
fuzz-jam-june.md.answer: Yes, fuzzing should be part of the code being shipped, and there is also a specific need for fuzzing the toolchain itself.

fuzz-jam-june.md.question: Where can someone learn more about the history and modern practices of fuzzing?
